analysis.bug_hypothesis: The recursive call in the gcd function passes arguments incorrectly as (a % b, b) instead of (b, a % b). This causes infinite recursion when a % b equals b.
analysis.trigger_condition: When a % b equals b, the recursion will never terminate, leading to infinite recursion.
analysis.expected_vs_actual: Expected: The gcd function should return the greatest common divisor of a and b. Actual: The function enters infinite recursion and does not terminate.
analysis.suggested_test_strategy: Write a pytest test case using gcd(35, 21) and assert that the result equals 7. This will reveal the infinite recursion in the buggy version and confirm the correct behavior in the fixed version.
